subject: Geography
title: Portolan Charts
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Portolan Charts.

hook.text: Portolan charts were medieval sea maps covered in a web of compass lines for navigation.

buildup.text: Italian and Catalan sailors drew coastlines with remarkable accuracy from direct observation.

discovery.text: They showed harbors, headlands, and hazards—everything a captain needed to stay alive.

twist.text: The interiors of continents were left blank. Sailors only cared about the coast.

climax.text: Portolans were so valuable that they were locked in ships' chests and guarded like treasure.

punchline.text: The most practical maps ever drawn left everything inland blank.

quiz.question: What were portolan charts primarily used for?
